title: ophrys sphegodes subsp. helenae
intro: Ophrys sphegodes subsp. helenae, also known as the bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is characterized by its distinctive, bee-like appearance and is native to certain regions of Greece and Turkey. Traditionally, the bee orchid has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to promote digestive health and reduce fever.
